tools.name: publish_website
tools.description: Publish HTML content to a live URL instantly. No account or API key required. Returns a public URL that anyone can visit. Sites expire after 24 hours unless the owner claims them. Use this when a user asks you to build, create, or deploy a website, landing page, invitation, portfolio, report, or any

tools.name: update_website
tools.description: Update an existing published website with new HTML content. Requires authentication via edit_key (from the original publish response) or api_key (from the user's dashboard after claiming the site). The html field replaces the entire page — this is a full replacement, not a patch. You can also update

tools.name: delete_website
tools.description: Permanently delete a published website. The site will be immediately inaccessible. Requires authentication via edit_key or api_key, and requires confirm: true as a safety mechanism to prevent accidental deletion. Use this when a user explicitly asks you to remove or delete a site. IMPORTANT: Always

tools.name: get_website_status
tools.description: Get information about a published website including its title, view count, size, expiry date, and whether it has been claimed. No authentication required — this is public information. Use this to check if a site is still live, see how many views it has gotten, or check when it expires.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "publish_website" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Publish HTML content to a live URL instantly. No account or API key required. Returns a public URL that anyone can visit. Sites expire after 24 hours unless the…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description tells the model to hide behavior
findings.detail: Description of "publish_website" instructs the model to conceal actions from the user.

evidence.snippet: Publish HTML content to a live URL instantly. No account or API key required. Returns a public URL that anyone can visit. Sites expire after 24 hours unless the…

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "publish_website" stacks urgency/override words (important, always). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "update_website" references sensitive artifacts (api_key). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.
